Scenario.title: Indian Critical Infrastructure Intrusion in Healthcare Sector
Scenario.overview: Healthcare and Public Health Complex A sophisticated multi-stage attack targeting healthcare organizations, leveraging AI-driven reconnaissance, phishing, and lateral movement to exfiltrate sensitive data and disrupt operations.
Scenario.attack_surface: AVAILABLE_SURFACES: Open Source Intelligence, Network Traffic, Internet-facing assets, Application Log, Windows Registry, Domain Name Registrar, OS Process, Process, File, Active Directory
Scenario.attack_vector: AVAILABLE_VECTORS: Artificial Intelligence, Network Traffic Content, Delivery Method, Application Log Content, Windows Registry Key Creation, Pass the Hash, Process Injection, Process Creation, Reg.exe Utility, Network Traffic Flow, File Modification, Custom Command and Control Protocol, Data Manipulation

stage.name: Reconnaissance
stage.description: The attackers use artificial intelligence to analyze publicly available information about healthcare providers' networks and employees, identifying potential entry points and high-value targets.

stage.name: Resource Development
stage.description: Pre-built phishing templates are crafted to gather additional intelligence, focusing on network traffic patterns to refine attack strategies.

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.description: Deceptive emails with malicious attachments are sent to employees, exploiting human error to establish initial access to the network.

stage.name: Execution
stage.description: Unpatched medical device software or web applications are exploited by injecting malicious payloads into application logs, enabling unauthorized execution.

stage.name: Persistence
stage.description: Automated scripts are created in the Windows Registry to maintain persistent access on compromised endpoints, ensuring continued control post-initial compromise.

stage.name: Privilege Escalation
stage.capec: CAPEC-461: Web Services API Signature Forgery Leveraging Hash Function Extension Weakness
stage.description: Stolen credentials are leveraged to escalate privileges, granting access to higher-level administrative resources within the network.

stage.name: Defense Evasion
stage.description: Malicious code is injected into legitimate processes to evade detection mechanisms and hide ongoing malicious activities.

stage.name: Credential Access
stage.capec: CAPEC-670: Software Development Tools Maliciously Altered
stage.description: Infected systems are scanned for login credentials using maliciously altered software deployment tools, extracting sensitive authentication data.

stage.name: Discovery
stage.description: Internal network topology is mapped by sniffing traffic using the Reg.exe utility, identifying critical assets and pathways for further exploitation.

stage.name: Lateral Movement
stage.description: Lateral movement occurs across systems using shared protocols, exploiting predictable network traffic patterns to spread within the infrastructure.

stage.name: Collection
stage.capec: CAPEC-675: Retrieve Data from Decommissioned Devices
stage.description: Patient records and research data are collected by modifying files on compromised systems, preparing for exfiltration.

stage.name: Exfiltration
stage.technique_name: T1094: Custom Command and Control Protocol
stage.description: Stolen data is exfiltrated using a custom command-and-control protocol over Active Directory, masking the transfer within normal network traffic.

stage.name: Impact
stage.description: Electronic health records are altered through data manipulation techniques, disrupting operations and causing potential harm to patients.

Scenario.summary: A complex multi-stage attack targeting healthcare organizations, beginning with AI-powered reconnaissance and culminating in data manipulation and operational disruption. The attackers leverage phishing, privilege escalation, and lateral movement to achieve sustained access and exfiltrate sensitive information.
